question: Compare the NuScale Power Module in Figure 1.2-4 with the cutaway in Figure 1.2-5. What additional information does the cutaway reveal?

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends provided for both I&C and piping and instrumentation diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must supply additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0002.title: Introduction
nuscale_ch01_0002.summary: The Introduction section outlines that this document is the Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for the NuScale Power modular nuclear plant, submitted as part of the standard design certification process under 10 CFR 52. It describes the design, safety analysis, and operational limits of the NuScale Power Module (NPM), a scalable, compact, and innovative small modular reactor system, highlighting key safety features such as passive shutdown, advanced containment, and digital controls.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section describes the inclusion of simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It references tables and figures listing and explaining the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at a NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the central placement and structural details of the Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a reinforced concrete, Seismic Category I structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool arranged in two rows of bays. The section also references figures for detailed layouts and notes that some buildings, like the administration and warehouse, are outside the FSAR scope.

nuscale_ch01_0032.title: Radioactive Waste Management System
nuscale_ch01_0032.summary: The section describes the radioactive waste management system at the NuScale Power Plant, noting that liquid, gaseous, and solid waste systems are detailed in Chapter 11, along with effluent monitoring. It also outlines the general arrangement of major structures, focusing on the Reactor Building (RXB), which is a reinforced, seismically designed concrete structure housing the nuclear power modules and providing radiation protection for personnel.

nuscale_ch01_0113.title: Figure 1.7-3b: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0113.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, detailing standard symbols and notations for valve positions, actuator types, and operational statuses. It explains abbreviations such as NO (normally open), NC (normally closed), FO (fail open), and FC (fail closed), and lists various actuator mechanisms and their representations on P&IDs. The legend ensures consistent interpretation of diagram elements throughout the documentation.

nuscale_ch01_0112.title: Figure 1.7-3a: Piping and Instrumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0112.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, detailing the standardized symbols and notations for various valves, vents, drains, fire safety equipment, and specialty items. It specifies that all vents and drains are represented as 0.75" normally closed valves unless otherwise noted, and includes explanations for system abbreviations and flow path indicators.

nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section explains the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified plant portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities of the COL applicant. The section also notes that COL applicants must address all COL information items and provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for symbols used in NuScale's Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the graphical representations for various instruments, sensors, and auxiliary devices. It includes symbols for sample probes, pressure and temperature sensors, flow measurement devices, and level indicators, ensuring standardized interpretation of P&ID drawings.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides a summary of the NuScale plant’s overall design, including key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling water, waste management, and auxiliary systems. It explains that each Combined License (COL) applicant must reference the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) and provide site-specific details where required, while distinguishing between certified design elements and conceptual design information (CDI). Security-related information is specially marked and withheld according to regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch01_0000.summary: This preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifying that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the document as needed for regulatory purposes. It also notes that proprietary information is protected, and any copies made by the NRC must include the appropriate copyright and proprietary notices.

nuscale_ch01_0135.title: Table 1.9-3: Conformance with NUREG-0800, Standard Review Plan (SRP) and Design Specific Review Standard (DSRS)
nuscale_ch01_0135.summary: This section presents Table 1.9-3, which summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with the regulatory criteria outlined in NUREG-0800 (Standard Review Plan) and the Design Specific Review Standard (DSRS). The table lists specific SRP and DSRS sections, their acceptance criteria, and indicates whether NuScale’s application conforms or if the criteria are not applicable, with brief comments explaining each determination.

nuscale_ch01_0005.title: Reactor Type
nuscale_ch01_0005.summary: The NuScale reactor is a small modular pressurized water reactor (PWR) featuring a passive safety design. Each module integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, surrounded by a steel containment vessel designed to withstand high pressures. A NuScale plant can include up to 12 modules, each producing 160 MWt (about 50 MWe), for a total net output of approximately 570 MWe.

nuscale_ch01_0067.title: Drawings and Other Detailed Information
nuscale_ch01_0067.summary: This section describes the inclusion of simplified instrumentation, control, electrical, and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It lists the types of diagrams provided, references legends for interpreting symbols, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must supply additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

edges.description: Section A provides detailed drawings and diagrams that visually clarify the design and instrumentation of plant systems, while Section B describes the functional features and utility services housed within the Central Utility Building, with the diagrams in Section A potentially illustrating or specifying the systems mentioned in Section B.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures, such as piping and instrumentation diagrams, that visually clarify design information referenced in Section B's general introduction to the NuScale Power Module and its safety analysis.

Section B is a subset of Section A, with Section A providing a general description of all types of drawings (including electrical, I&C, and mechanical), while Section B specifically focuses on electrical and instrumentation and control drawings, thus Section A provides the broader context for Section B's details.

Section A provides detailed drawings and diagrams, including instrumentation and controls, that visually clarify design information, while Section B describes the general arrangement and layout of major structures and equipment, with both sections using figures to illustrate different levels of detail about the NuScale Power Module.

Section A provides detailed drawings and diagrams, such as piping and instrumentation, that can visually clarify the design features shown in Section B's general plant descriptions and figures, thereby offering more specific information about the arrangement and function of systems like the radioactive waste management system.

Section A provides specific details about plant features and structures such as the Central Utility Building, which are part of the general plant description introduced in Section B's overview of the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report.

Section B provides visual and schematic details (such as instrumentation and control drawings) that clarify and supplement the general plant features described in Section A, allowing for a more detailed understanding of the systems mentioned.

Section B provides a general overview of the plant's major structures and equipment layout, while Section A specifies the particular features and utility systems housed within the Central Utility Building, thereby detailing one of the structures referenced in Section B.

Section B references the general arrangement of major structures and equipment, which provides context for the plant features described in Section A, connecting the physical layout to the specific utility and safety functions.
Section B explains that figures such as Figure 1.2-4 and Figure 1.2-5 provide visual clarification of design information referenced in the general description provided in Section A.

Section A provides the overall purpose and scope of the FSAR, while Section B offers specific details about the general arrangement of major plant structures and equipment, thereby specifying the content introduced in Section A.

Section B references specific figures (such as Figure 1.2-2) and provides detailed descriptions of plant layout and systems, which are the basis for the visual representations compared in the analysis question, while Section A provides the general context and purpose of the document.

Section A provides detailed drawings, including instrumentation and control diagrams, that visually clarify design information, while Section B describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment, with both sections referencing figures that illustrate different aspects of the NuScale Power Module, thereby allowing a comparison where the cutaway in Figure 1.2-5 reveals internal details not visible in the general arrangement of Figure 1.2-4.

Section A provides general information about the use of figures for electrical and instrumentation drawings, while Section B references specific figures (such as Figure 1.2-2) that depict the general arrangement of major plant structures, connecting them through their use of visual representations to clarify plant design.

Section A provides a general overview of the arrangement of major structures and equipment, while Section B focuses specifically on the radioactive waste management system, with both sections referencing the same figures and chapters for detailed information, thus Section A's general description encompasses and leads into the more specific details found in Section B.
Section A provides the legend and status definitions for valve symbols used in piping and instrumentation diagrams, which are referenced and applied in the diagrams described in Section B.
Section A provides detailed legends for interpreting piping and instrumentation diagrams, which are necessary for understanding the specific plant features and systems described in Section B.

Section A provides detailed diagram legends and operational statuses for instrumentation and piping, which are referenced and utilized in the general plant description and safety analysis context outlined in Section B.

Section A provides the legend and symbol definitions necessary to interpret the piping and instrumentation diagrams referenced in Section B, which presents the actual diagrams and their explanatory context.

Section A provides standardized diagram symbols and legends for interpreting piping and instrumentation diagrams, which are necessary for understanding the detailed plant features and utility systems described in Section B.
Section A provides specific diagram legends and notation details that are used to interpret the general plant design descriptions introduced in Section B.

Section A identifies which structures, systems, and components are included in the certified design, while Section B provides detailed drawings (such as piping and instrumentation diagrams) that visually clarify the design information for those components.

Section A outlines the interface requirements and identifies which structures, systems, and components are part of the certified design, while Section B provides detailed descriptions of specific plant features (such as the Central Utility Building) that may be depicted or further clarified in the cutaway view of Figure 1.2-5, thus Section B specifies details for the general framework established in Section A.

Section A references specific figures and sections that identify and represent the certified design boundaries and components, while Section B introduces the overall purpose and scope of the FSAR, including the description of the design and its safety analysis, thus providing the broader context for the details in Section A.

Section A provides the specific legend symbols and their meanings used in the piping and instrumentation diagrams described in Section B, enabling interpretation of the diagrams referenced in Section B.

Section A provides detailed legends for instrumentation and auxiliary devices used in plant diagrams, which helps interpret the general descriptions of plant utility systems and features listed in Section B.

Section A provides specific diagram legends used in detailed drawings referenced throughout the FSAR, while Section B introduces the FSAR and its purpose, establishing the context in which such detailed figures (like those in Section A) are used.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant design, while Section B offers detailed drawings (such as piping and instrumentation diagrams) that visually clarify and specify the systems described in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant's systems and structures, while Section B specifies detailed features and functions of a particular structure (the Central Utility Building) mentioned in the general description.

Section A provides a detailed general description of the plant's design and systems, while Section B introduces the purpose and regulatory context of the document, establishing the framework within which Section A's details are presented.
Section A provides additional valve status codes and operational details that specify and expand upon the general valve and vent/drain symbols and conventions introduced in Section B.

Section A provides diagram legends and conventions necessary to interpret detailed drawings like those referenced in Section B, which discusses interface requirements and references figures representing the certified design boundaries.

Section A and Section B both provide legends for interpreting piping and instrumentation diagrams, with Section A focusing on valve statuses and Section B detailing auxiliary and accessory device symbols, together enabling comprehensive understanding of P&ID drawings.
Section A provides detailed diagram legends and valve status conventions that specify how to interpret the general system descriptions and diagrams summarized in Section B.

Section A provides detailed diagram legends necessary to interpret piping and instrumentation diagrams, which are referenced in Section B as part of the certified design documentation required for interface with site-specific design in the COL application.

Section A and Section B both provide legends for interpreting symbols on Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams, with Section A focusing on valves and flow paths while Section B details auxiliary devices and instrumentation, together offering a comprehensive guide to understanding P&ID figures.

Section A provides specific diagram legends and notation details that are used to interpret the general plant systems and features described in Section B, enabling a more precise understanding of the plant's instrumentation and piping as referenced in the general description.

Section A provides detailed diagram legends for instrumentation and auxiliary devices that are referenced in the general plant description of Section B, thereby specifying the symbols and components used in the broader system overview.

Section B provides detailed drawings and diagrams that visually clarify the design information introduced in Section A's general description, offering additional specifics such as component positions and instrumentation not present in the preface.

Section B provides detailed descriptions of plant features and utility systems that may be visually represented or further clarified by the cutaway view in Figure 1.2-5, which is referenced in the analysis question alongside the general module depiction in Figure 1.2-4.

Section A outlines regulatory conformance requirements for the NuScale design, while Section B describes the provision of detailed drawings (such as piping and instrumentation diagrams) that visually clarify the design and support the demonstration of such conformance.

Section A documents the NuScale design's conformance with regulatory criteria, while Section B provides detailed descriptions of specific plant features, with the latter offering concrete examples that support the general compliance claims made in the former.

Section A lists the regulatory criteria and conformance status for the NuScale FSAR, while Section B introduces the FSAR and its purpose, establishing that the conformance table in Section A is part of the broader documentation described in Section B.
Section B provides detailed drawings, such as piping and instrumentation diagrams, that visually clarify and supplement the general containment vessel design described in Section A.

Section A describes the containment vessel's design and regulatory classification, while Section B lists plant utility features housed in a separate building, showing that the cutaway in Figure 1.2-5 reveals internal containment details not addressed in the general plant utility description.
Section A provides specific technical details about the NuScale containment vessel that are part of the general plant design and safety analysis overview introduced in Section B.

Section A provides general information about the use of drawings for visual clarification, while Section B specifies that piping and instrumentation diagrams are among the types of drawings referenced in Section A.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify design information, which can include cutaway views like Figure 1.2-5, while Section B describes plant features and structures such as the Central Utility Building, so the cutaway in Figure 1.2-5 (referenced in Section A) reveals internal details of plant features discussed in Section B.
Section A provides detailed drawings and figures, such as those referenced in Figures 1.2-4 and 1.2-5, which visually clarify the general plant description introduced in Section B.

Section A provides the general context and copyright information for the NuScale Design Certification Application, while Section B details how the application conforms to specific regulatory criteria, with both sections connected by their reference to the same application document and its compliance with NRC requirements.

Section B provides specific technical details about the NuScale containment vessel that are visually represented and further clarified by the cutaway illustration referenced in Section A, allowing for a more comprehensive understanding of the module's internal structure.

Section B explains that figures such as Figure 1.2-4 and Figure 1.2-5 provide visual clarification of design information, which supports the analysis question about what additional details the cutaway (Figure 1.2-5) reveals compared to the standard module (Figure 1.2-4).
Section A documents the NuScale design's conformance with regulatory criteria, while Section B provides specific technical details about the containment vessel that are relevant to those criteria.

Section A documents regulatory conformance requirements for the NuScale design, while Section B explains that figures (such as Figures 1.2-4 and 1.2-5) provide detailed visual information to clarify the design, thus supporting the regulatory review described in Section A.

Section B provides detailed drawings and visual clarifications, such as cutaways, that supplement the general containment vessel description in Section A by revealing internal components and arrangements not specified in the text.
